description_long: This is a double-leaf metal gate with a symmetrical design, featuring vertical bars and two large circular motifs at the center of each leaf. The upper section of the gate is adorned with decorative scrollwork, adding an ornate touch to its otherwise utilitarian structure. The lower portion of the gate is solid, likely made from sheet metal, and shows signs of weathering and rust, suggesting outdoor use. The gate is relatively narrow in depth, as seen from the side views, and appears to be constructed primarily from steel or iron. A latch mechanism is visible at the meeting point of the two leaves, indicating manual operation. The overall finish is matte with a slightly rough, weathered texture, and the color is predominantly gray with patches of rust.
description: A weathered double-leaf metal gate with ornate scrollwork, circular motifs, and a solid lower panel, finished in gray with visible rust patches.
description_view_0: Front view showing both leaves, circular motifs, scrollwork, and the latch at the center.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the gate's narrow depth and flat profile.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, showing the same decorative and structural features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again highlighting the thinness and flatness of the gate.
